epigraph.text:
Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, 
or with the breadth of his hand marked off the heavens?

content.text:
Perimeter tells us the distance around a shape; area tells us the space 
inside. These measurements are essential for countless practical 
applications, from buying carpet to planning gardens to building houses.

content.title: Perimeter
content.content:
The **perimeter** of a polygon is the total 
distance around it—the sum of all side lengths.

content.title: Finding perimeters
content.problem:
Find the perimeter of each shape:

1. A triangle with sides 5 cm, 7 cm, and 9 cm

2. A rectangle with length 8 m and width 5 m

3. A square with side 6 ft

content.title: Perimeter Formulas
content.content:
- **Any polygon:** Add all side lengths

- **Rectangle:** $P = 2l + 2w$ (or $P = 2(l + w)$)

- **Square:** $P = 4s$

content.title: Area
content.content:
The **area** of a figure is the amount of surface it 
covers, measured in square units (such as cm², m², ft², or in²).

content.text: To find area, we count how many unit squares fit inside the shape.

content.content:
Throughout Scripture, measurements matter. God gave Noah precise 
dimensions for the ark (Genesis 6:15), Moses exact specifications for 
the tabernacle (Exodus 26), and Ezekiel detailed measurements for the 
temple (Ezekiel 40-42). Area calculations help us steward space wisely.

content.title: Rectangle Area
content.content:
The area of a rectangle with length $l$ and width $w$ is:
\[
A = l \times w = lw
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Square Area
content.content:
The area of a square with side $s$ is:
\[
A = s \times s = s^2
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Area of rectangles
content.problem:
Find the area:

1. A rectangle 12 cm by 5 cm

2. A square with side 9 m

3. A rectangular room 15 ft by 12 ft

content.title: Triangle Area
content.content:
The area of a triangle with base $b$ and height $h$ is:
\[
A = \frac{1}{2} \times b \times h = \frac{bh}{2}
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.text: The height must be perpendicular (at a right angle) to the base.

content.title: Area of triangles
content.problem:
Find the area of a triangle with:

1. Base 10 cm and height 6 cm

2. Base 8 m and height 5 m

content.title: Scripture
content.content:
The triangle's area formula ($\frac{1}{2}bh$) reveals that a triangle 
is exactly half of a rectangle with the same base and height. This 
connection shows how geometric truths build upon each other, just as 
"precept upon precept, line upon line" (Isaiah 28:10).

content.title: Parallelogram Area
content.content:
The area of a parallelogram with base $b$ and height $h$ is:
\[
A = b \times h = bh
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
Note: The height is the perpendicular distance between the bases, not 
the slanted side!

content.title: Parallelogram area
content.problem: A parallelogram has base 14 cm and height 8 cm. Find its area.

content.title: Carpeting a room
content.problem:
A rectangular room is 15 feet by 12 feet. Carpet costs \$3 per square 
foot. How much will it cost to carpet the room?

content.title: Fencing a garden
content.problem:
A rectangular garden is 20 m long and 8 m wide. How much fencing is 
needed to enclose it?
content.solution:
Perimeter $= 2(20) + 2(8) = 40 + 16 = 56$ m

56 meters of fencing is needed.

marginNotes.text:
By night I went out through the Valley 
Gate... examining the walls of Jerusalem.

Then he measured the temple; it was a 
hundred cubits long.

The portico at the front of the 
temple was twenty cubits long.

marginNotes.author: Archimedes
marginNotes.text:
Give me a lever long enough and a fulcrum on 
which to place it, and I shall move the world.

marginNotes.author: Leonardo da Vinci
marginNotes.text:
Realize that everything connects to 
everything else.

marginNotes.author: Euclid
marginNotes.text: The whole is greater than the part.
